_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-15R Atropine (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-15R: Atropine — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Crashing bradycardia (severe hemodynamic instability from bradycardia)
Organophosphate poisoning or nerve agent exposure
contraindications: Tachycardia (do not give for bradycardia in high-degree AV block — transcutaneous pacing preferred)
precautions: Minimum pediatric dose: 0.1 mg (doses below 0.1 mg paradoxically worsen bradycardia)
Atropine is not effective for infranodal (Mobitz II / complete heart block) bradycardia — pacing is first-line
Nerve agent exposure: pralidoxime (9-39R) should accompany atropine when available
Use 2 mg IM/IV per nerve agent auto-injector kit per Chart A and Chart B protocol
expected_effects: Increased heart rate by blocking vagal tone
Improved AV conduction in vagally-mediated bradycardia
In nerve agent/organophosphate poisoning: reversal of muscarinic effects (bronchospasm, secretions, bradycardia)
side_effects: Dry mouth, urinary retention, blurred vision
notes: Bradycardia: 1 mg IV q3-5 minutes, max 3 mg total
Pediatric minimum dose: 0.1 mg to prevent paradoxical bradycardia
Nerve agent: 2 mg IM/IV per nerve agent auto-injector kit; Chart A and Chart B dosing apply; pralidoxime should accompany when available

dosing.dose: 1 mg IV q3-5 minutes; max 3 mg total
dosing.notes: For vagally-mediated bradycardia; less effective for infranodal blocks — consider pacing

dosing.dose: 0.02 mg/kg; minimum dose 0.1 mg; maximum single dose 0.5 mg; may repeat once
dosing.notes: Do NOT give less than 0.1 mg — paradoxical bradycardia risk

dosing.dose: 2 mg per nerve agent auto-injector kit per Chart A and Chart B
dosing.notes: Use nerve agent kit auto-injectors; administer pralidoxime concurrently when available; repeat per nerve agent protocol
